stories.title: World Cup Ticket Sales Struggle Despite High Demand Claims
stories.desc: With under 40 days until kickoff, many group-stage games still have unsold tickets at exorbitant prices, with USA vs Paraguay opener tickets ranging from $1,120 to $6,050, contradicting FIFA's predicted demand.

stories.title: Record-Breaking Panini Album Launches with 980 Stickers
stories.desc: Panini officially launched its biggest-ever World Cup sticker album on April 28 at Wembley Stadium, featuring 980 stickers across 112 pages, including 68 special edition stickers with stars like Messi and Ronaldo.

stories.title: Panini Album Goes On Sale With New 7-Sticker Packs
stories.desc: The 2026 sticker collection officially went on sale April 30 at major retailers including Walmart, Target and Amazon, with albums costing $5 and individual 7-sticker packets priced at $2.

stories.title: World Cup 2026 Drives Uneven Tourism Demand Across Host Nations
stories.desc: The FIFA World Cup 2026 is reshaping tourism across the US, Canada, and Mexico with rising demand, soaring hotel prices, and connectivity gaps as host nations prepare for the 48-team tournament.

stories.title: Collectors Face Biggest Challenge Yet with 48-Team Album
stories.desc: Statistically, collectors may need more than 1,000 packets costing around £1,000 to complete the album. Panini is hosting live swap shop events across the UK in May to help fans trade duplicates.

stories.title: Group C Features Brazil, Morocco, Scotland and Haiti
stories.desc: Tournament groups have been finalized following the December 2025 draw, with Group C featuring Brazil, Morocco, Scotland, and Haiti in what promises to be a competitive matchup.
